summary: Completed end-to-end frontend testing of SEO Jalwa Public Marketing site + User Dashboard (phase 2). 11 public routes load, both auth pages load without navbar, signup/login flows authenticate and redirect to /dashboard, AuthRedirect protects auth pages when logged-in, DashboardLayout protects dashboard when logged-out (redirects to /login), all 6 dashboard pages render with sidebar nav working, CookieBanner appears and persists consent to localStorage, and the critical localStorage pricing bridge (seo_jalwa_admin key) is verified working - custom prices $99/$299/$599 render correctly when set, and defaults $79/$199/$499 render when key is absent. Light theme + Syne font + #1D9E75 green primary all verified on HomePage. No console errors, no framer-motion errors.

ui_bugs.issue: After clicking dash-logout-btn, user lands on /login instead of / (home) as the spec requires. Cause: logout() sets isAuthenticated=false which triggers DashboardLayout's `if (!isAuthenticated) return <Navigate to="/login" replace />` BEFORE the subsequent `navigate('/')` can execute. Order of operations is wrong.
ui_bugs.fix_suggestion: In handleLogout, call navigate('/') FIRST, then logout() — or move the redirect-to-login guard so it does not fire on the same tick as logout. Example: const handleLogout = () => { navigate('/'); setTimeout(() => logout(), 0); };

action_items: Fix DashboardLayout logout so it redirects to '/' (home) instead of '/login'. Reorder so navigate('/') runs before logout() state update, or guard the Navigate so it doesn't fire on the click tick.
critical_code_review_comments: UserContext.getPricing reads from localStorage key 'seo_jalwa_admin' which is the SAME key the AdminContext uses for admin auth. Admin login writes {isAuthenticated, username}; pricing edit writes {pricing: {...}}. There is a real risk of one overwriting the other depending on admin context's setState pattern. Recommend verifying AdminContext merges with existing key contents (read → merge → write) rather than overwriting. Worth a focused review of /app/frontend/src/admin/context/AdminContext.jsx.

UserContext useEffect persists state on every mount even when state is null/default — first visit to ANY public page writes {isAuthenticated:false,user:null,cookieConsent:false} into seo_jalwa_user immediately. Functionally fine but could be skipped on first mount.
DashboardLayout navItems has a 'Billing' entry pointing to '/dashboard/settings?tab=billing' but is sliced off via navItems.slice(0,-1) — dead code; remove or render it.
AuthRedirect wrapper exists for /login, /signup, /forgot-password but there is no analogous redirect for an authenticated user landing on / (home) — spec is silent on this so this may be intentional.
PricingPage feature list only shows allFeatures.slice(0, 10) in the cards (the comparison table below shows all 15). Acceptable design choice but worth confirming with PRD.

success_rate.frontend: 97% (35/36 verified — only logout redirect destination is off)

test_credentials: Dummy mode — any email/password works. Used test@jalwa.com / test1234 for signup with name='Test User', website='https://test.com'. No real auth.
seed_data_creation: Set localStorage.seo_jalwa_admin = {pricing:{starter:{monthlyPrice:99,annualPrice:89},growth:{monthlyPrice:299,annualPrice:249,popular:true},agency:{monthlyPrice:599,annualPrice:499}}} to verify pricing bridge from admin → public pricing page.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase-2 (Public+Dashboard) verified. Key testids: login-page, signup-page, pricing-page, dashboard-layout, dash-page-title, dash-logout-btn, dash-nav-home/jalwa-pulse/jalwa-write/jalwa-publish/jalwa-post/settings, login-email-input, login-password-input, login-submit-btn, signup-name-input, signup-email-input, signup-password-input, signup-website-input, signup-submit-btn. To clear auth: localStorage.removeItem('seo_jalwa_user'). Pricing bridge key: 'seo_jalwa_admin' with {pricing:{starter,growth,agency}}. CookieBanner: 'Accept' button writes cookieConsent:true into seo_jalwa_user. PricingPage cards use framer-motion whileInView so must scroll into viewport before reading innerText (page.content() may still show them but innerText needs scroll).
verified_flows: All 11 public routes (/, /features, /pricing, /integrations, /about, /blog, /blog/ai-seo-2026-complete-guide, /contact, /privacy, /terms, /cookies) load without errors
All 3 auth routes (/login, /signup, /forgot-password) load without PublicLayout navbar
Signup with {name, email, password, website} → navigates to /dashboard and writes seo_jalwa_user with isAuthenticated:true
Login with any email/password → navigates to /dashboard
AuthRedirect: logged-in user visiting /login or /signup is redirected to /dashboard
Protected dashboard: unauthed visit to /dashboard redirects to /login (DashboardLayout's Navigate guard)
All 6 dashboard pages render with DashboardLayout (home, pulse, write, publish, post, settings)
All 6 sidebar nav items navigate correctly and active state shows green (#E1F5EE bg + #1D9E75 text via Tailwind arbitrary classes)
Public Navbar: Sign in → /login; Start free → /signup; logo links to /
CookieBanner appears on first visit, Accept button persists cookieConsent:true into seo_jalwa_user
PricingPage default values render $79 / $199 / $499 when seo_jalwa_admin has no pricing field
PricingPage reads custom pricing from seo_jalwa_admin localStorage and renders $99 / $299 / $599 correctly — bridge confirmed working
Light theme verified: body background rgb(255,255,255), 18 elements with .font-syne class on HomePage, #1D9E75 (rgb 29,158,117) used as bg/border/text on multiple elements
Framer Motion: HomePage hero animation present, no console errors related to framer-motion

rca of the issue.logout_redirect: RCA: In DashboardLayout.handleLogout, the order is `logout()` then `navigate('/')`. logout() calls setState which marks isAuthenticated=false. React schedules a re-render of DashboardLayout, which on next render hits `if (!isAuthenticated) return <Navigate to='/login' replace />`. The Navigate component runs during render and replaces the current URL with /login BEFORE the imperative navigate('/') call can take effect (or after, but Navigate replace wins because the component re-renders again). Result: user ends up on /login instead of /. Reproduction: login → click dash-logout-btn → URL ends with /login. Mitigation: navigate('/') BEFORE logout(), or wrap logout() in setTimeout(()=>logout(),0) so the navigation completes first, or guard the Navigate redirect with a flag that distinguishes 'just-logged-out' from 'never-authed'.
